title: Pet Import Guide: Canada
description: Complete guide to importing your dog or cat to Canada. Import requirements, quarantine, breed restrictions, and step-by-step process.
name: Canada
overview: Importing a pet to Canada is moderate. Here's what you need to know.

import_requirements.import_permit: Personal pet imports (1-2 animals) do not require an import permit. Commercial imports (for sale, breeding, adoption, fostering, or more than 2 dogs) require CFIA import permit.

sections.heading: Import requirements for Canada
sections.body:
**Microchip:** Required (ISO 11784/11785 recommended)

**Rabies Vaccination:** Required

**Quarantine:** Not required (penalty quarantine if non-compliant)

**Import Permit:** Personal pet imports (1-2 animals) do not require an import permit. Commercial imports (for sale, breeding, adoption, fostering, or more than 2 dogs) require CFIA import permit.

**Health Certificate:** Required. Valid 10 days.

sections.heading: Breed restrictions in Canada
sections.body: Governed by: No federal breed-specific legislation. Provincial and municipal BSL.

faqs.question: What vaccinations does my pet need to enter Canada?
faqs.answer: At minimum, a rabies vaccination is required. Required Your pet must also be microchipped before vaccination.

faqs.question: Which airlines fly pets to Canada?
faqs.answer: Multiple airlines serve pet cargo to Canada. Check our route-specific guides for airline details on your particular origin-destination pair.
